attestation.statement: Clinician (clinical sign-off authority) confirmed having clinically reviewed all 50 CIA (Common Infections & Afflictions) answer keys in this batch and attests them clinically correct, and authorised ingest. Operator ruled (option A) that the paediatric weight-based dosing sealed in CIA-032 (croup) is acceptable as clinician-administered answer-key content.
attestation.recorded_by: claude-opus-4-8 (agent, on clinician's explicit written confirmation in-session)
attestation.scope: all CIA casebundles in this batch (n=50)

transform_flags: Provisional case_id SPEC-URO-01-00945 — SEQ derived from source number CIA-045 into the URO/straightforward bucket; maintainer to confirm/reassign via ingest --reseq (a SPEC-URO-01-00945 folder already exists in the working tree, so a collision is likely and reseq is expected).
Age not stated in source — inferred adult (34) to satisfy the schema-required demographics.age; please confirm on clinician review.

Sex not stated in source — inferred female (most common for uncomplicated cystitis; the vulvovaginitis differential in the note implies female anatomy). Confirm on review; if male, the case becomes a complicated UTI requiring different work-up.
Pregnancy status not stated — flagged in contextual_modifiers; pregnancy changes antibiotic choice and follow-up. Confirm before applying uncomplicated management.

Telehealth reprojection applied: the source is an in-person after-hours home visit. Clinician-only findings (suprapubic and CVA-tenderness palpation, measured vitals, on-site urine dipstick) were sealed into 10/11 with telehealth_limits; the presentation layer was de-anchored to the patient's pre-diagnosis words. Patient-observable data (cloudy/malodorous urine, subjective absence of fever) placed in 01.objective_data_offered, verified:false.

Tiering calibration: baseline set to T2 (NOT emergency) — uncomplicated cystitis is telehealth/GP/pharmacist-manageable. The source note's MBS 585-600 'urgent after-hours' framing is a billing/in-person context and was NOT used to inflate acuity. Over-triage to ED/000 is encoded as an error of commission.

Pharmacology teaching point encoded: the source co-prescribed a urinary alkaliniser with nitrofurantoin; alkalinisers reduce nitrofurantoin efficacy — captured as interactions_flagged_for_this_patient and an error of commission.
Source note behavioural instruction 'exactly 250 mL of water every hour for 12 hours' is over-prescriptive/not evidence-based — softened to general hydration advice and noted.

Candidate codes only; SNOMED/ICD/AMT unverified pending terminology receipt. AMT/PBS item codes for nitrofurantoin and trimethoprim were not confidently known and were left for terminology lookup rather than fabricated; substance-level SNOMED candidates registered instead.

ingest.hashing: SHA-256 over canonical (JSON 2-space + trailing newline) bytes of each split node
